title: RabbitMQ 4.3 Delayed Retries, Priorities, and Consumer Timeouts · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A messaging pairing room predicts publisher, exchange, queue, consumer, ownership, and failure transitions before assembling the first RabbitMQ 4.3 feature and fallback compatibility plan for rabbitmq 4.3 delayed retries, priorities, and consumer timeouts. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of RabbitMQ 4.3 feature and fallback compatibility plan while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum delayed-retry policies for returned or failed deliveries with explicit delay, eligibility, attempt, and compatibility evidence.
Compare native delayed retries with TTL and DLX cycles by rewrite cost, loss and duplicate windows, head-of-line effects, topology, and version support.
Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum 32 strict priority levels with bounded use, starvation evidence, prefetch interaction, and per-priority monitoring.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Delayed Retry Policy
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop messaging team handles case 8401 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum retry, priority, and timeout behavior on version and valid budgets; record causal telemetry without message IDs as metric labels; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum delayed-retry policies for returned or failed deliveries with explicit delay, eligibility, attempt, and compatibility evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Delayed Retry Policy.
